commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=247beac1702341891ea2350f2a58847eb3eee897)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds setup-wizard dependency-recovery UI for app-managed WSL gateways and moves WSL host/terminal helpers into OpenClaw.Connection.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a focused path: trigger a missing-dependency wizard error with a live session, click Restart gateway, and have the WSL control command fail before the gateway actually restarts; the PR’s added path disconnects before sending `wizard.cancel`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for real WSL behavior proof, and maintainers should review the refreshed merge result after the source-level blockers are fixed.

target.title: Add WSL gateway dependency-recovery to setup wizard
target.body:
## What & why

When the gateway **config wizard** (provider/model/OAuth onboarding) fails because a required CLI/tool is missing on an **app-managed WSL gateway**, the wizard used to dead-end on a generic error. This PR adds in-product recovery so the user can fix and continue without leaving the app.

The wizard error state now offers:

- **Copy install command** — surfaces the install command parsed out of the error text.
- **Open terminal** — drops the user into the WSL distro to run the fix.
- **Restart gateway** — restarts the distro, reconnects, and re-enters the config wizard right where they left off.

Recovery affordances are gated on the gateway being an **app-managed WSL distro** (`GatewayHostAccess.CanControlWslGateway`) and the failure looking like a missing dependency — so they never appear for unrelated errors or gateways we don't control.

## Changes

- **Wizard recovery UI** — new `DependencyRecovery` panel in `WizardPage.xaml` + handlers in `WizardPage.xaml.cs` (`MaybeShowDependencyRecovery`, copy / open-terminal / restart).
- **Missing-dependency heuristic** — new `WizardDependencyError` that detects likely "tool not installed" failures and extracts the install command from the error text.
- **WSL helpers relocated** — `WslCommandRunner`, `WslGatewayController`, `GatewayHostAccess`, `GatewayTerminalLauncher` move from `OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI/Services` into `OpenClaw.Connection` so the SetupEngine UI can consume them (history-preserving renames).
- **Docs** — `docs/ONBOARDING_WIZARD.md` notes the recovery flow.

## Hardening (from a dual-model review)

- Restart handler claims the operation generation and locks the UI **synchronously before the first await**, and re-checks the generation after each await, so a double-click can't fire a stale restart.
- `WslGatewayController` only reports *"distro not registered"* when enumeration **definitively** lacks it; an empty list (probe failure/timeout) **fails open** so the real error surfaces.
- Gateway restart uses a **2-minute timeout** (vs the 30s default) so a slow cold-distro restart isn't killed as a spurious timeout.
- Install-command extraction **preserves multi-package commands** when wrapped in matching backticks/quotes.

## Validation

- `./build.ps1` — ✅ all projects (ARM64, warnings-as-errors clean)
- `OpenClaw.Shared.Tests` — ✅ 2065 passed / 0 failed / 29 skipped
- `OpenClaw.Tray.Tests` — ✅ 983 passed / 0 failed (+ new tests for the fail-open enumeration and multi-package extraction)

## Screenshots

The new `DependencyRecovery` panel in the wizard error state, showing the parsed install
command with a **Copy** button plus **Open terminal** and **Restart gateway** actions
(only rendered for an app-managed WSL gateway hitting a missing-dependency error).

**Dark**

![WSL gateway dependency recovery — dark](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bkudiess/openclaw-windows-node/pr-assets/wsl-gateway-recovery/wsl-recovery-dark.png)

**Light**

![WSL gateway dependency recovery — light](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bkudiess/openclaw-windows-node/pr-assets/wsl-gateway-recovery/wsl-recovery-light.png)
target.author: bkudiess
